system_classification.rationale: Express HTTP server with 19 routes, session-based auth, MongoDB persistence, HTML/JSON responses. Strong web-api signals (HTTP methods, browser-oriented templates, RESTful patterns) with identity-access as secondary (login/signup/logout, admin/user roles).

reasons: broad-attack-surface (5 populated categories: ingress, identity, parsing, state, sinks)
multiple-data-stores (MongoDB collections: users, allocations, memos, contributions, benefits)
external-integrations (SSRF via needle, redirect via URL param)

source_registry.reason: Documents A1-Injection: SSJS eval(), NoSQL $where, CRLF log injection with explicit attack vectors and code locations
Documents A2-Broken Auth: weak password storage/comparison, user enumeration, session fixation
Documents A3-XSS: autoescape disabled, reflected/stored XSS vectors
Documents A4-IDOR: allocations userId from URL param bypass
Documents A5-Security Misconfig: helmet disabled, insecure cookies, hardcoded secrets

A6-Sensitive Data Exposure content (HTTP vs HTTPS, secure cookies) is already fully covered by leads from A2 (L13-L15 passwords), A5 (L16-L18 security misconfig/cookies), and A3 (XSS stealing session data). No unique actionable content not already registered.
Documents A7-Missing Function Level Access Control: benefits without isAdmin
Documents A8-CSRF: csrf protection commented out
Documents A9-Insecure Components: marked 0.3.5 XSS, general dependency risks
Documents A10-Unvalidated Redirects: /learn endpoint with user-controlled URL
Documents ReDoS: profile.js regex catastrophic backtracking with explicit trigger input
Documents SSRF: /research endpoint making user-controlled HTTP requests
Express router only — routes to tutorial view pages, no vulnerability logic
HTML layout template only — structural markup, no vulnerability disclosure
Cypress test code — verifies tutorial page navigation, not a vulnerability source

lead_registry.claim_summary: SSJS Injection via eval() in code/app/routes/contributions.js — eval(req.body.preTax/afterTax/roth) parses user input as JavaScript code
NoSQL Injection via $where in code/app/data/allocations-dao.js — threshold parameter injected into JavaScript expression
CRLF Log Injection in code/app/routes/session.js — userName unsanitized in console.log
IDOR in code/app/routes/allocations.js — userId taken from URL params instead of session
XSS via Swig autoescape:false in code/server.js — all template variables unescaped
XSS via marked 0.3.5 library — vulnerable markdown parser allows XSS in memos
Missing Function Level Access Control — /benefits routes lack isAdmin middleware
CSRF Protection missing — csurf require commented out in code/server.js
Open Redirect in /learn — res.redirect(req.query.url) without validation
SSRF in /research — needle.get(req.query.url + req.query.symbol) without URL validation
ReDoS in code/app/routes/profile.js — regex /([0-9]+)+\#/ vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking. Trigger: long digit string without # suffix
Session Fixation — login does not call req.session.regenerate()
Weak Password Storage — passwords stored as plaintext in code/app/data/user-dao.js
Weak Password Comparison — plaintext === instead of bcrypt.compareSync
User Enumeration — different error messages for invalid user vs invalid password
Missing Security Headers — helmet/nosniff commented out, no CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options
Insecure Session Cookies — no httpOnly, no secure flag, no generic cookie name
Hardcoded Secrets — cookieSecret and cryptoKey in code/config/env/all.js
Insecure Dependencies — marked 0.3.5 (XSS vuln), mongodb ^2.1.18, needle 2.2.4
Missing Function Level Access Control — /benefits route lacks isAdmin middleware allowing non-admin users to access admin functionality

coverage_plan.claim_scope: risk-weighted dynamic-test coverage; not Bug recall or proof that the target is vulnerability-free

direction_matrix.rationale: eval(), NoSQL $where, ReDoS regex, body-parser — multiple injection entry points at input boundary.
IDOR (allocations userId from URL), missing isAdmin on benefits, user enumeration. Static analysis with authorization-matrix and negative-space techniques.
Contribution limits (<30%) enforced only via eval()-parsed values. Static analysis performed.
MongoDB upsert patterns, no transaction support visible.
SSJS eval() injection, NoSQL $where injection, CRLF log injection, marked XSS, stored XSS in memos.
Open redirect /learn, SSRF /research, missing CSRF, missing security headers.
No file upload, download, or path manipulation functionality exists. Verified by reviewing all 19 routes and view templates.
body-parser JSON/URL-encoded, eval() integer parsing, marked markdown parsing, Swig HTML templating.
Plaintext password storage/comparison, session fixation, user enumeration, weak password policy, insecure cookies.
Plaintext passwords, hardcoded cookieSecret/cryptoKey, no HTTPS.
CRLF log injection, differential error messages (user enumeration), stack traces in error handler.
Helmet disabled, CSRF disabled, HTTP not HTTPS, insecure session config, weak password policy, hardcoded secrets.
marked 0.3.5 (known XSS), mongodb ^2.1.18, needle 2.2.4. Version analysis performed.
HPP warning in profile.js, mass assignment in benefits, no rate limiting.
ReDoS catastrophic backtracking CONFIRMED (92s CPU). Dynamic test executed via Python regex.

specialty_obligations.rationale: Assessed all HTTP/browser security aspects via code review: CSRF disabled (code/server.js:7), no CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/X-Content-Type-Options (helmet commented out code/server.js:38-65), session cookies lack httpOnly/secure flags (code/server.js:91-101), HTTP only-no HTTPS (code/server.js:145), open redirect (code/app/routes/index.js:70-73), SSRF (code/app/routes/research.js:15-16).

coverage_plan.coverage_gap_reason: Node.js runtime unavailable — cannot execute dynamic tests for 19/20 candidates. Only ReDoS test executable via Python regex engine. 1/15 directions have true dynamic test coverage.

changes: Downgraded business-logic: tested→partial (dynamic execution blocked by Node.js)
Downgraded api-abuse: tested→partial (no HTTP request execution)
Adjusted calculated_percent to reflect actual dynamic testing (88.33%)
rebalance.decision_summary: Recalculated risk-weighted coverage after breadth pass. 3 medium and 1 high direction downgraded due to Node.js runtime unavailability. Only 1/15 directions (concurrency-resource) has true dynamic test via Python regex. Coverage gap documented in quality-tools.json.

coverage.unavailable_reason: Node.js runtime not installed — c8/nyc/istanbul require node executable and npm packages. Verified via quality_tools.py detect showing all node-based coverage tools unavailable.

direction_assessments.rationale: ReDoS regex mutation: removed redundant quantifier ([0-9]+)+→([0-9]+). Killed — fixed regex processes 30-char input in <1ms vs 92s.

mutation.unavailable_reason: Full mutation testing blocked — Node.js/Stryker-js unavailable. Only 1 manual regex mutation performed.

notes: The Agent must select at least three relevant strategies.
Complex projects should target at least four relevant strategies.
This run must not inherit findings or generated tests from another run.
Temporarily quarantine answer-bearing material until independent first-pass candidates are fixed.
Then review every discovered source and validate every actionable lead.
Confirmed project leads count as confirmed Bugs while retaining provenance.
Assess all 15 bug directions and prioritize them by system archetype.
A complete run targets 95 percent risk-weighted dynamic-test coverage, not 95 percent Bug recall.
Measure native runtime code coverage when available and prove key target paths executed.
Run bounded targeted mutation testing only in an isolated copy; complete runs require no unresolved critical mutant and at least 80 percent mutation score.
Log the initial execution, normal control, and every rerun separately.
